title: Awesome Little Side Table
summary: A little side table with a big impact.
description: A little side table with a big impact. This project is a great one for a novice builder. You’ll go from a small pile of pressure treated lumber to a neat table in a few hours.

steps.title: Build the Main Frame
steps.body: 1. Join the two long (1′ 4.5″) stretchers to two legs using wood glue, biscuits if you’d like, and a fastener of your choice (either deck screws or finishing nails). 2. Clamp the parts together while assembling to keep everything flat and easy to manage.

steps.title: Build Secondary Base Frame
steps.body: 1. Join the remaining two legs to the four short (7.5″) stretchers with wood glue and decking screws. 2. Clamp the subassemblies together to keep the parts together.

steps.title: Build Base with Frame Assemblies
steps.body: 1. Center the two leg subassemblies on the original leg assembly. 2. Join these parts together with glue and decking screws.

steps.title: Cut the Table Top Rail Pieces
steps.body: 1. Cut the table top rail pieces, with a 45-degree angle at both ends, as indicated above. 2. Prep a total of 4 pieces.

steps.title: Attach Table Top Rail Pieces
steps.body: 1.) Cut the table top rail pieces, with a 45-degree angle at both ends, as indicated above. 2.) Prep a total of 4 pieces.

steps.title: Cut and Attach Top of Table
steps.body: 1. Lay four 1’10” boards for the top on a flat surface to get the most visually appealing configuration. 2. Start by attaching the middle two boards centered on the base, using wood glue and decking screws or finishing nails.

steps.title: Sand, Stain, Enjoy
steps.body: 1.) Sand any rough edges. 2.) Paint, stain, or seal as you’d like. 3.) Enjoy!
